title: Telos Holocron
content:

{{Quote|All that we know, we shall share freely with you. All we ask in return is that you keep an open mind, and use this knowledge for the benefit of only one being: yourself. It is not out of greed that we ask this of you, but out of respect. Of all those who might have stumbled upon this device, no one is more deserving of our knowledge than you.|Darth Sidious, holocron gatekeeper|<ref name="JvS" />}}
The '''Telos Holocron''' was a [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]] of atypical design crafted by adherents to the [[Sith/Legends|Sith Order]] at some point prior to the [[Golden Age of the Sith]]. Intended to be a repository of the lore and wisdom that defined their philosophy, the holocron was passed down through generations of [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] after its creation, many of whom imbued their knowledge within the device. A number of the Lords became [[Gatekeeper/Legends|gatekeepers]] of the Telos Holocron, lending their image and voice to the repository as a simulacrum that future users could interface with. In [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], the [[New Jedi Order]] discovered the [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocron]] and quarantined it within their [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|academy]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]]. While investigating the device, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Tionne Solusar]] learned that its most recent gatekeeper was [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]].

The Sith holocron contained testimonials from numerous Sith Lords from varying points in galactic history, including [[Ajunta Pall]] of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Exiles]], [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]], [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], founder of the [[Order of the Sith Lords]], [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]] and Sidious himself. While a number of the entries were in regards to the history and plight of the Sith, others were more focused on [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] practices and teachings of the arcane [[Force power/Legends|powers]] that could be accessed through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. While it was in his possession, Sidious made several additions to the repository based on his own experiences, and contributed addenda to the entries of past users in order to update the holocron with more modern references. In the year of its discovery, Master Solusar was able to unlock the device and transcribe its contents into a [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force|manual on the Force]] that she was crafting for future generations of [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]].

==Properties==
{{Quote|Although this holocron is not pyramidal and has no external markings or glyphs, it is unquestionably of Sith origin.|Tionne Solusar's report on the device|<ref name="JvS" />}}
The Telos Holocron was of a highly atypical design, defying the conventions of both [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocrons]]. Where most [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]] created by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] were pyramidal in shape, the [[Telos IV|Telos]] artifact was amorphous, characterized by an asymmetrical base of [[red]] matter surrounded by spiky protrusions. Set within the base was a vertical formation, the apex of which held a clear [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]]. The device appeared to eschew the intricate crystalline matrices of other holocrons, and lacked the glyphs and outward markings that so often characterized Sith holocrons. Despite its unconventional appearance, it was obvious to members of the [[New Jedi Order]] that the Telos Holocron was an artifact of the Sith.<ref name="JvS" />

Like all holocrons, the Telos Holocron was a storage device imbued with the power of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. As a repository of protected information regarding the Sith Order and its teachings, the Telos Holocron was both guarded and overseen by [[Gatekeeper/Legends|gatekeepers]]—manifestations of the thoughts and personae of previous users. These gatekeepers were more than simple virtual intelligences—they were able to interface with and react to users in real-time, responding to their queries as if they were true beings. While most Sith holocrons only manifested their gatekeepers audibly, the Telos Holocron provided a visual simulacrum of its guardians as well. The contents of the device were only accessible to [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitives]]; however, unlike other holocrons, the Telos Holocron was capable of detecting the persuasion of those who attempted to use it and deny access to [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who would possibly use the information within to undermine the goals of the Sith.<ref name="JvS" />

==Contents==
{{Quote|No, I am not the first keeper of this Holocron. It has been passed down through the ages by many Sith Lords, most of whom left records for your erudition.|Darth Sidious|<ref name="JvS" />}}
By [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], the Telos Holocron contained information and testimonials from over five thousand [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of Sith history, as well as almost all force powers pertaining to the dark side. The various [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] who possessed the holocron over the ages contributed to its knowledge base, leaving records of historical events, analysis of Sith practices, or lessons on the manipulation of the Force. In [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]], Darth Sidious, [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] and [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Galactic Emperor]], added his persona to the Telos Holocron to become the gatekeeper of the device. Sidious's simulacrum appeared as the Dark Lord did in his youth, under the pretense that elderly teachers can seem antiquated and condescending.<ref name="JvS" />

The gatekeeper greeted users with a statement encouraging them to explore the powers at their disposal, regardless of any warnings issued by past teachers, and praised them for being strong enough to access the information within the holocron. It went on to state that while the information within the holocron was freely available to the user, the contributors' wish offered it under a single stipulation—that the learner use the information for the benefit of only themselves. The simulacrum closed the greeting by claiming that the first priority for any student looking to comprehend the ways of the Sith was understanding the history of their order.<ref name="JvS" />

===The Conquest of Sith Space===
[[File:Ziost_egtf.jpg|thumb|left|230px|Ajunta Pall on the Sith world [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]]]]
{{Quote|The Jedi Knights intended to let us fall like a single raindrop into an endless desert, but they only sent us to a greater glory. While they decay in their stodgy trappings and power-inhibiting rules of conduct, we grow stronger every day. It is only a matter of time before we shall thank them in an appropriate manner.|Ajunta Pall|<ref name="JvS" />}}
One of the earliest contributors to the Telos Holocron was [[Ajunta Pall]], who, as Sidious noted, was one of the earliest Sith Lords and a combatant in the [[Battle of Corbos|final battle]] at [[Corbos]] during the [[Hundred-Year Darkness/Legends|Hundred-Year Darkness]]. Pall addressed his record to his descendants and those of his comrades, who had taken up the title of Sith Lords and established an [[Sith Empire/Legends|empire]] around their newly-forged religion. In the entry, the Sith Lord advised listeners on how to govern the Sith Empire, urging them not to only rely on superior power and the Force, but also ambition. He continued into an extended explanation of the Empire's origin, in which he stressed that the rulers of the Sith should be thankful not only to their forefathers, but to the Jedi Order as well.<ref name="JvS" />

As Pall explained, the Sith Order and its empire was born out of the Jedi Order and the schism it suffered in the Hundred-Year Darkness. After discovering methods of [[Sith alchemy/Legends|twisting lifeforms]] through the use of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]], a sect of the Jedi Order came to be known as [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]], and were persecuted by the orthodox [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. After the defeat of the Dark Jedi at Corbos, Pall and his comrades were [[Exile (punishment)/Legends|exiled]] from known space and purged from the records of the Jedi Order. After being loaded onto unarmed vessels and sent to uncharted regions of [[Realspace/Legends|space]], the [[Exiles]] landed on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], home to the primitive [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]]. Pall provided a first-hand account of how the Exiles used their [[Sith sorcery/Legends|sorcery]] to oppress and dominate the natives, eventually earning the title "Lords of the Sith."<ref name="JvS" />

Pall closed his entry with an anecdote on an attempt by some of his fellow Sith Lords to exact revenge on the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] and his opposition to the plan. After leaving the [[Sith Worlds/Legends|space]] that had been organized into the first Sith Empire, the activist Lords were never heard from again. Nevertheless, Pall stated his belief that the day would come for a renewed Jedi-Sith conflict.<ref name="JvS" />

===The Sith Code===
{{Quote|The tenets of the Sith are more than just words to be memorized. Learn them, understand them. They will lead you to the true power of the Force: the power of the dark side.|[[Qordis]]|<ref name="JvS" />}}
A Dark Lord of the [[Brotherhood of Darkness/Legends|Brotherhood of Darkness]] known as [[Qordis]] added a short entry to the Telos Holocron in which he recited and explained the [[Code of the Sith/Legends|Sith Code]]. According to Qordis, the tenets of the code were more than simple phrases for memorization and recitation; indeed, understanding them was the key to unlocking the deepest powers of the Force. Qordis indicated that the Code had been in use since the Exiles took up the title of Lords of the Sith, and included a copy of the text. Sidious added an addendum to the Dark Lord's entry, noting that Qordis was one of the more powerful Lords of [[Skere Kaan|Kaan's]] Brotherhood of Darkness, yet still fell to [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] during the latter's purge of the Sith Order at the close of the [[New Sith Wars]].<ref name="JvS" />

==History==
===Quarantined by the Jedi===
{{Quote|Despite my own cautions, I acknowledge that the Telos Holocron might contain information that will help the Jedi during any potential future dealings with the Sith, and support Luke Skywalker's decision to let Tionne study it.|Jedi Master Kam Solusar|<ref name="JvS" />}}
The holocron was discovered on [[Telos IV]] by the [[New Jedi Order]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]], shortly after their rediscovery of the [[Great Holocron/Legends|Great Holocron]]. Nearly half of the [[Jedi High Council (New Jedi Order)|Master's Council]] argued that the Holocron should be destroyed due to the threat of it falling into the wrong hands.<ref name="JvS" />

[[Kam Solusar]] was suspicious of the device, but studied it with his wife [[Tionne Solusar]] with the support of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. Four Jedi were assigned to watch over Tionne as she delved into the Telos Holocron, watching for any signs of potential corruption or manipulation of the user.<ref name="JvS" />

One of the Holocron's [[Gatekeeper/Legends|Gatekeepers]] was [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], who obtained the Holocron at some point during his reign and left his dark legacy to future generations.<ref name="JvS" />

==Sources==
